title: Ballad Stanzas.
content:
I knew by the smoke, that so gracefully curled
        Above the green elms, that a cottage was near.
    And I said, "If there's peace to be found in the world,
        "A heart that was humble might hope for it here!"
    It was noon, and on flowers that languished around
        In silence reposed the voluptuous bee;
    Every leaf was at rest, and I heard not a sound
        But the woodpecker tapping the hollow beech-tree.

    And, "Here in this lone little wood," I exclaimed,
        "With a maid who was lovely to soul and to eye,
    "Who would blush when I praised her, and weep if I blamed,
        How blest could I live, and how calm could I die!

    "By the shade of yon sumach, whose red berry dips
        "In the gush of the fountain, how sweet to recline,
    "And to know that I sighed upon innocent lips,
        "Which had never been sighed on by any but mine!"
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "Ballad Stanzas."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
